event: FCC Covered List firmware update prohibition creates stranded asset crisis Jan 2027 — gray zone entities (Anzu, SkyRover, Cogito) cannot patch security vulnerabilities or regulatory bugs after firmware freeze, forcing operators to choose between non-compliance and operational risk
evidence_basis: FCC equipment authorization freeze (Dec 2025) blocks new Chinese drone imports. Existing gray zone flags show Anzu/SkyRover/Cogito use DJI-signed firmware with adversary crypto keys. FCC Covered List rules prohibit firmware updates touching adversary infrastructure after Jan 2027. No primary source confirms firmware exemption carve-out.
probability_basis: Strong inference from FCC authorization freeze + existing teardown evidence of DJI firmware control. No Jan 2027 firmware exemption documented.
drivers: FCC Covered List enforcement timeline approaching Jan 2027
Gray zone entities retain adversary firmware signing keys per teardown evidence
No documented firmware update exemption pathway

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: FCC firmware freeze + adversary crypto key control = stranded asset wave Q4 2026

probability_basis: Credible signal from Ohio DFR closed vendor list and federal enforcement momentum. 8-12 states is educated estimate, not documented count.
drivers: Ohio DFR pilot excludes gray zone entities (precedent set Jan 2026)

Estimated 40-80 platforms: CENTCOM mine clearance doctrine typically 20-40 UUVs per choke point, doubled for Iranian threat density.
